what_it_does: Implements a file-discovered registry for narrative GUIDE entries: each guide is declared as a module-level GUIDE dict in a guides/<id>.py file, validated for identity, content, target, and mandatory grounding, then exposed through a dict-like GuideRegistry whose read() returns the guide content for guide://<id>.
observations: Module docstring defines the design rationale and laws (no-hardcoding, reuse-don't-parallel, fail-loud, grounding-mandatory, the floor).
Defines GUIDE_FIELDS as the closed schema for guide declarations.
GuideEntry is a dataclass that stores the raw spec and exposes typed accessors for id, content, target, grounded_from, source_hash, label, and description.

_build_guide performs strict validation: id must equal filename, content/target must be non-empty strings, grounded_from must be a non-empty list of non-empty strings, and unknown fields are rejected.
GuideRegistry discovers .py files (excluding underscore-prefixed) in given directories, imports them via _load_module from runtime.skills, and registers any module declaring GUIDE.
Provides discover(), rediscover() (clear then discover), register(), read(), and dict-like __getitem__/__contains__/__iter__/get methods.
read() is intentionally named read, not resolve, because resolving a guide is a plain content read with no dispatch.
source_hash is optional and stored but not computed or compared in this file.
standouts: Mandatory grounding gate: a guide must declare a non-empty grounded_from list of source addresses or fail loud ('abort on cold').
Identity-by-filename enforcement: GUIDE['id'] must equal the module name.
Mirrors the SkillRegistry surface while carrying a richer, distinct schema (one registry pattern, own row shape).
Content is the resolvable value; guide://<id> is a read, not a routed dispatch.
conventions: Follows the one-registry pattern with a dict-like API.
Reuses the schema-agnostic _load_module helper from runtime.skills instead of inventing a new loader.
Fail-loud validation: malformed declared entries raise rather than being silently skipped.
Honors 'the floor': reading a guide returns declared content, no further resolution.
Heavy docstring-as-design-record explaining laws and review-derived decisions.
concerns: source_hash is declared but never validated, computed, or freshness-checked in this file.
discover() silently skips directories that do not exist and files without a GUIDE dict, which is consistent with the pattern but means drift in configured paths goes unreported.
register() overwrites an existing entry without warning, so duplicate ids across directories silently last-write-win.
No synchronization: rediscover() clears and rebuilds entries, which could race with concurrent reads.
Optional fields like source_hash, label, and description are not type-checked beyond dataclass/GuideEntry accessors.
notes: A fail-loud, file-discovered registry that treats narrative how-to guides as first-class, grounded, addressable rows.
questions: How and where is source_hash computed, and where is freshness against current sources checked?
What directories are passed to discover()/rediscover() at runtime?
How does runtime/cognition.py implement the guide:// dispatch branch mentioned in the seam?
Are grounded_from addresses verified to exist elsewhere, or is the list only syntactically validated here?
What is the intended lifecycle for re-authoring a guide when source_hash indicates staleness?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a file-discovered guide registry with strict validation and a read-only content resolution surface.
intention_signals: To make human-readable how-to guides as discoverable, verifiable, and addressable as skills/roles, ensuring every guide is authored from real sources and cannot be invented or silently malformed.
novelty: The mandatory grounding gate and optional source_hash freshness field for narrative guides, plus treating a learner-facing how-to as an addressable registry entry resolved by guide://<id>.
contribution: A reusable, validated registry implementation for guide entries that enforces identity-by-filename, mandatory grounding, and a read-only resolution surface, which a fused system could keep to maintain discoverable, source-anchored documentation.
summary_for_embedding: File-discovered registry for narrative guide entries declared in guides/<id>.py modules, validating identity, content, target, and mandatory source grounding before resolving guide://<id> to its markdown content.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a fail-loud, grounded, addressable how-to layer for system components that learners can read without resolving or dispatching.

symbols.does: Dataclass representing a discovered guide row with typed accessors.
symbols.description: Stores the raw GUIDE declaration in spec and exposes id, content, target, grounded_from, plus optional source_hash, label, and description via properties. It is the typed row object returned by the registry.

symbols.does: Property returning the optional source_hash from the raw spec.
symbols.description: Provides typed access to the declared source_hash, which is intended to track freshness of the grounded sources at authoring time; returns None if absent.

symbols.does: Property returning a human-facing short name.
symbols.description: Returns the declared label if present, otherwise falls back to the guide id, giving operators a readable name without requiring an explicit label.

symbols.does: Property returning the optional one-line description.
symbols.description: Returns the declared description string, or None if the guide did not provide one.

symbols.does: Validates a raw GUIDE dict and builds a GuideEntry.
symbols.description: Enforces the closed schema: id must equal filename, content and target must be non-empty strings, grounded_from must be a non-empty list of non-empty strings, and unknown fields are rejected. Raises loudly on any malformed declared entry.
Maintains a mapping of guide ids to GuideEntry objects, discovers them from guides/<id>.py files, and exposes dict-like lookup plus a read() method that returns content.
Creates a fresh registry with no discovered guides, ready for discover() or manual register() calls.

symbols.does: Scans directories for guide modules and registers them.
symbols.description: Iterates sorted .py files (excluding underscore-prefixed) in each provided directory, imports them via _load_module, and registers any module declaring a GUIDE dict.

symbols.does: Rebuilds the registry from the filesystem.
symbols.description: Clears all entries and re-runs discover() so removed guide files unregister and new files register.

symbols.does: Validates and stores a guide declaration under a given name.
symbols.description: Calls _build_guide and assigns the resulting GuideEntry to self.entries[name], overwriting any existing entry without warning.

symbols.does: Returns the content of a guide by id.
symbols.description: Looks up the guide id and returns its declared content string; raises loudly if the id is not registered, enforcing registry-is-truth and no fabrication.
Allows reg[guide_id] to retrieve the full GuideEntry object, matching the dict-like registry surface.

symbols.does: Dict-like membership test for a guide id.
symbols.description: Returns True if the guide id is present in the registry entries, supporting id in reg checks.
Yields the keys of the entries dictionary, supporting for id in reg iteration.
Returns the GuideEntry for the given id if present, otherwise returns the supplied default value.
